# group-ride

> A Discord & Telegram bot to organise group cycling rides — propose rides, manage participants, send day-before and hour-before reminders, attach iCal files, and import routes from Komoot, Strava, or Garmin.

group-ride is open-source and self-hostable. It supports two messaging adapters (Discord and Telegram) selected via a single environment variable. A single Docker image covers both.

## Getting started

- [Overview](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/): What group-ride is and how it works
- [Installation](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/installation/): Quick-start overview and Docker Compose setup
- [Install on Discord](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/adapters/discord/installation/): Step-by-step guide to set up the Discord bot
- [Install on Telegram](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/adapters/telegram/installation/): Step-by-step guide to set up the Telegram bot
- [Configuration](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/configuration/): All environment variables — adapter, tokens, database, timezone

## Adapters

- [Discord — Overview](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/adapters/discord/): Slash commands, button interactions, forum threads, member events
- [Discord — Installation](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/adapters/discord/installation/): Step-by-step setup guide
- [Telegram — Overview](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/adapters/telegram/): Commands, topics, differences from Discord
- [Telegram — Installation](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/adapters/telegram/installation/): Step-by-step setup guide

## Reference

- [Architecture](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/architecture/): Hexagonal architecture, domain model, ports and adapters
- [Commands](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/docs/commands/): Full reference for all Discord slash commands, Telegram commands, and interactive buttons
- [Troubleshooting](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/docs/troubleshooting/): Common issues grouped by category
- [Deployment](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/deployment/): Scaleway instance, Docker Compose, CI/CD, backups
- [Changelog](https://group-ride.slashgear.dev/docs/docs/changelog/): Release history

---

## Environment variables

All configuration is via environment variables. None have defaults except where noted.

| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ADAPTER` | no | `discord` | Which messaging adapter to use: `discord` or `telegram` |
| `DISCORD_TOKEN` | Discord only | — | Bot token from Discord Developer Portal |
| `DISCORD_CLIENT_ID` | Discord only | — | Application ID from Discord Developer Portal |
| `DISCORD_GUILD_ID` | Discord only | — | Server (guild) ID where the bot operates |
| `DISCORD_ANNOUNCEMENTS_CHANNEL_ID` | Discord only | — | Channel ID for ride announcements |
| `DISCORD_FORUM_CHANNEL_ID` | Discord only | — | Forum channel ID for ride threads |
| `TELEGRAM_TOKEN` | Telegram only | — | Bot token from @BotFather |
| `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` | Telegram only | — | Group chat ID where the bot operates |
| `DATABASE_URL` | no | — | PostgreSQL connection URL (e.g. `postgres://user:pass@host/db`). If absent, SQLite is used. |
| `DATABASE_PATH` | no | `./data/group-ride.db` | SQLite file path (ignored when `DATABASE_URL` is set) |
| `TZ` | no | system timezone | Timezone for reminder scheduling, e.g. `Europe/Paris` |
| `LANG` | no | `en` | Bot message language: `en` (English) or `fr` (French) |

---

## Architecture

group-ride uses hexagonal architecture (Ports & Adapters). The domain layer has zero knowledge of Discord, Telegram, SQLite, or PostgreSQL.

```
src/
├── domain/
│   ├── ride.ts           # Ride entity + CreateRideInput type
│   ├── errors.ts         # RideNotFoundError, RideNotActiveError, AlreadyMemberError
│   └── ports/
│       ├── messaging.port.ts   # MessagingPort interface
│       └── ride.repository.ts  # RideRepository interface
├── services/
│   ├── ride.service.ts        # Business logic (propose, join, leave, cancel, update)
│   ├── scheduler.service.ts   # Reminder and close logic, runs every 60 s
│   ├── ical.ts                # Generates .ics buffer for calendar attachments
│   ├── map-generator.ts       # Renders static route map PNG from GPX
│   └── importer/              # Route importers: komoot, strava, garmin, gpx
└── adapters/
    ├── messaging/
    │   ├── discord/           # Discord.js implementation of MessagingPort
    │   └── telegram/          # grammY implementation of MessagingPort
    └── database/
        ├── sqlite/            # bun:sqlite implementation of RideRepository
        └── postgres/          # Bun SQL (template-tag) implementation of RideRepository
```

**Rule**: `src/services/` and `src/domain/` must never import from `src/adapters/`.

---

## Domain model

```ts
interface Ride {
  id: string            // UUID
  threadId: string | null
  proposerId: string    // Discord snowflake or Telegram user ID (string to avoid precision loss)
  proposerName: string
  date: Date
  name: string | null
  meetingTime: string | null   // "HH:MM" format
  meetingPoint: string
  distanceKm: number | null
  elevationGain: number | null
  elevationLoss: number | null
  level: "easy" | "moderate" | "hard" | null
  gpxUrl: string | null
  externalUrl: string | null
  notes: string | null
  status: "active" | "cancelled" | "closed"
  pinnedMessageId: string | null
  reminderDaySent: boolean
  reminderHourSent: boolean
  createdAt: Date
}
```

---

## Commands reference

### Discord (slash commands + buttons)

| Command / Button | Description |
|---|---|
| `/newride` | Opens a modal form to propose a new ride |
| `/rides` | Lists all upcoming rides with Join buttons |
| `/help` | Shows usage instructions (ephemeral) |
| **Join this ride** button | Registers the user for a ride |
| **Leave** button (in thread) | Removes the user from a ride |
| **Cancel ride** button (in thread) | Cancels the ride and notifies members |
| **✏️ Edit** button (in thread) | Opens an edit modal (proposer or admin only) |

### Telegram (bot commands + inline buttons)

| Command / Button | Description |
|---|---|
| `/newride` | Starts a guided conversation to propose a ride |
| `/rides` | Lists all upcoming rides with Join buttons |
| `/edit` | Starts a guided conversation to edit an existing ride |
| `/help` | Shows usage instructions |
| **Join** button | Registers the user for a ride |
| **Leave** button | Removes the user from a ride |
| **Cancel ride** button | Cancels the ride and notifies members |

---

## Scheduler

The `SchedulerService` runs a `tick()` every 60 seconds:

- **Hour-before reminder**: sent when the ride is between 45 and 75 minutes away (window accounts for tick interval)
- **Day-before reminder**: sent when the ride date is tomorrow (midnight-to-midnight comparison in local TZ)
- **Close ride**: triggered 24 hours after the ride date — updates status to `closed`, posts a closing message, and locks the thread

Reminders are idempotent (`reminderDaySent`, `reminderHourSent` flags in the DB). The scheduler never double-sends.

---

## Route importers

Paste a URL when proposing a ride — group-ride auto-fills distance and elevation:

| Platform | What is extracted |
|---|---|
| Komoot | Distance, elevation gain/loss, GPX track URL |
| Strava | Distance, elevation gain |
| Garmin Connect | Distance, elevation gain/loss |
| GPX file upload | Distance, elevation gain/loss, static map preview PNG |

---

## Database

Two database adapters, selected automatically:

- **SQLite** (default): `bun:sqlite`, file at `DATABASE_PATH`. Good for single-server deployments.
- **PostgreSQL**: activated when `DATABASE_URL` is set. Uses Bun's native SQL template-tag client. Migrations live in `src/adapters/database/postgres/migrations/`.

Both implement the same `RideRepository` port — the rest of the codebase is database-agnostic.

---

## Runtime

- **Runtime**: [Bun](https://bun.sh) ≥ 1.3
- **Docker image**: `ghcr.io/slashgear/group-ride` (multi-arch: `linux/amd64` + `linux/arm64`)
- **Health check**: writes `Date.now()` to `/tmp/healthcheck` every 30 s; Docker checks file age
- **Graceful shutdown**: SIGTERM / SIGINT closes the PostgreSQL connection pool before exit

---

## Development

```bash
# Install dependencies
bun install

# Run the bot (with hot reload)
bun dev

# Run tests
bun test

# Format + lint (run before committing)
bun run fmt:check
bun run lint
bun run typecheck

# Start the documentation site
bun run website
```

For local development with Docker:
```bash
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up
```

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/slashgear/group-ride/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for the full setup guide.

---

## Technology stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Runtime | Bun |
| Discord adapter | discord.js v14 |
| Telegram adapter | grammY |
| Database (SQLite) | bun:sqlite (built-in) |
| Database (Postgres) | Bun SQL template tag (built-in) |
| Logging | pino |
| Documentation site | Astro + Starlight |
| Formatter | oxfmt |
| Linter | oxlint |
| CI | GitHub Actions |
| Container registry | GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io) |
